figures.caption: Fig. 2. Illustration of the context-dependent path with 𝜆-conditioned modulation introduced into the DCVC framework. We further extend the modulation mechanism to the spatiotemporal context modeling process: layer-wise feature modulation is inserted into the Contextual Encoder and Contextual Decoder, while an additional 𝜆-conditioned scaling is applied to the warped context feature obtained by motion compensation, followed by Context Refinement to generate bitrate-dependent contextual representations.
Fig. 4. Rate-distortion comparison of the lambda-modulated single-model baseline on the HEVC Class B/C/D/E and UVG datasets.

Fig. 3. Budget-constrained RD-optimized adjustment controller. The controller takes budget-state features and causal coding statistics as inputs, predicts a bounded ΔGRU log 𝜆𝑡 , and refines the base 𝜆 (base)
Fig. 7. Example of mini-GOP budget alignment under the proposed budget-constrained adjustment controller.
Fig. 1. Overall framework of the proposed feedback-driven rate control method for learned video compression. A feedback controller first generates the base control signal 𝜆 (base)
Fig. 8. Subjective quality comparison. From left to right: original frame with the selected region, cropped ground truth, baseline reconstruction, and reconstruction by the proposed method.

Fig. 5. Frame-wise bitrate comparison on two representative sequences under a common target bitrate. For each sequence, the left panel shows the DCVC-𝜆 baseline and the right panel shows the proposed PI control result. The proposed method tracks the target bitrate more stably through online 𝜆 updates.
